blocks.normalized_text: heavenly father we come before you today with a deep sense of responsibility
blocks.raw_text: Heavenly Father, we come before You today with a deep sense of responsibility.
blocks.source_text: Heavenly Father, we come before You today with a deep sense of responsibility.

blocks.normalized_text: we ask for your wisdom lord the kind of wisdom that seeks only what is true and what is right as senator-judges may we listen carefully reason clearly and decide justly may we never lose sight of the fact that our role here is not to serve any interest but the interest of the filipino people and the integrity of our institutions we pray for fairness in every step of this process may the truth be heard may the evidence speak for itself and may justice whatever form it takes be arrived at with honesty and clear conscience
blocks.raw_text: We ask for Your wisdom. Lord; the kind of wisdom that seeks only what is true and what is right. As Senator-Judges, may we listen carefully, reason clearly, and decide justly. May we never lose sight of the fact that our role here is not to serve any interest but the interest of the Filipino people and the integrity of our institutions. We pray for fairness in every step of this process. May the truth be heard, may the evidence speak for itself, and may justice, whatever form it takes, be arrived at with honesty and clear conscience.
blocks.source_text:
We ask for Your wisdom. Lord; the kind of wisdom that seeks only what is true and 
what is right. As Senator-Judges, may we listen carefully, reason clearly, and decide 
justly. May we never lose sight of the fact that our role here is not to serve any interest 
but the interest of the Filipino people and the integrity of our institutions. We pray for 
fairness in every step of this process. May the truth be heard, may the evidence speak 
for itself, and may justice, whatever form it takes, be arrived at with honesty and clear 
conscience.

blocks.normalized_text: we also pray for our nation we know that moments like this can divide us may this process difficult as it is bring us closer to the kind of philippines we all want to live in-one where no one is above the law and where every filipino can trust that justice is real
blocks.raw_text: We also pray for our nation. We know that moments like this can divide us. May this process, difficult as it is, bring us closer to the kind of Philippines we all want to live in- one where no one is above the law and where every Filipino can trust that justice is real.
blocks.source_text:
We also pray for our nation. We know that moments like this can divide us. May this 
process, difficult as it is, bring us closer to the kind of Philippines we all want to live in- 
one where no one is above the law and where every Filipino can trust that justice is real.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a commended the presiding officer for his prudence in handling the matter and emphasized that senator-judges should refrain from asking questions that could prejudice the proceedings he agreed that striking the prosecution s earlier remarks from the record would serve little purpose since the proceedings had already been televised
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) commended the Presiding Officer for his prudence in handling the matter and emphasized that Senator-Judges should refrain from asking questions that could prejudice the proceedings. He agreed that striking the prosecution’s earlier remarks from the record would serve little purpose since the proceedings had already been televised.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) commended the Presiding Officer for his prudence in handling the 
matter and emphasized that Senator-Judges should refrain from asking questions that could 
prejudice the proceedings. He agreed that striking the prosecution’s earlier remarks from the record 
would serve little purpose since the proceedings had already been televised.

blocks.normalized_text: he maintained that the respondent s counsel should be afforded the same opportunity previously extended to the prosecution with reasonable latitude to address both the question raised and matters relating to its affirmative defenses
blocks.raw_text: He maintained that the Respondent’s counsel should be afforded the same opportunity previously extended to the prosecution, with reasonable latitude to address both the question raised and matters relating to its affirmative defenses.
blocks.source_text:

previously extended to the prosecution, with reasonable latitude to address both the question 
raised and matters relating to its affirmative defenses.

blocks.normalized_text: she underscored the importance of strict adherence to the rules stating that orderly proceedings depend on their consistent application she explained that raising procedural objections and calling the attention of the presiding officer whenever the rules appear to have been violated are necessary to uphold due process and ensure the proper administration of justice
blocks.raw_text: She underscored the importance of strict adherence to the Rules, stating that orderly proceedings depend on their consistent application. She explained that raising procedural objections and calling the attention of the Presiding Officer whenever the Rules appear to have been violated are necessary to uphold due process and ensure the proper administration of justice.
blocks.section_title: WITHDRAWAL OF PREVIOUS MOTION AND REMARKS OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (P)
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) withdrew her previous motion, agreeing with the Presiding 
Officer’s ruling. She underscored the importance of strict adherence to the Rules, stating that 
orderly proceedings depend on their consistent application. She explained that raising procedural 
objections and calling the attention of the Presiding Officer whenever the Rules appear to have 
been violated are necessary to uphold due process and ensure the proper administration of justice.
blocks.speaker_raw: Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) withdrew her previous motion, agreeing with the Presiding Officer’s ruling

distracting others.

blocks.raw_text: So, Your Honor, please, we caimot constrain a witness to answering a ‘yes’ or a ‘no.’ The witness should be able to qualify. blocks.normalized_text: i would like to remind the senators-judges to be cautious in making counsels for either party make conclusions of law and fact instead of presenting evidence through a witness they will present or through direct cross redirect or recross because if he answers senator-judge hontiveros that would effectively place on record a conclusion of fact and law not drawn from a witness which they can very well do so during their closing remarks and when i allow him to speak by way of repartee to the peroration yesterday and discussion yesterday by counsel for the prosecution but other than that as agreed with some of the senator-judges we would try to limit the answers of the counsels for the parties with your indulgence senator-judge hontiveros please
blocks.raw_text: I would like to remind the senators-judges to be cautious in making counsels for either party make conclusions of law and fact instead of presenting evidence through a wimess they will present, or through direct, cross, redirect, or recross. Because if he answers, Senator-Judge Hontiveros, that would effectively place on record a conclusion of fact and law, not drawn from a witness which they can very well do so during their closing remarks and when I allow him to speak, by way of repartee, to the peroration yesterday and discussion yesterday by counsel for the prosecution. But other than that, as agreed with some of the senator-judges, we would try to limit the answers of the counsels for the parties. With your indulgence, Senator-Judge Hontiveros, please.
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer. I would like to remind the senators-judges to be cautious in making 
counsels for either party make conclusions of law and fact instead of presenting evidence through 
a wimess they will present, or through direct, cross, redirect, or recross. Because if he answers, 
Senator-Judge Hontiveros, that would effectively place on record a conclusion of fact and law, not 
drawn from a witness which they can very well do so during their closing remarks and when I allow 
him to speak, by way of repartee, to the peroration yesterday and discussion yesterday by counsel 
for the prosecution. But other than that, as agreed with some of the senator-judges, we would 
try to limit the answers of the counsels for the parties. With your indulgence, Senator-Judge 
Hontiveros, please.

2. The DOJ Certification dated February 5, signed by Prosecutor Bandong, identifying 
seven grounds for finding the complaint insufficient for the conduct of a preliminary 
investigation;
3. The Revised Affidavit of Authentication dated February 10, which had already been 
provided by the staff, as well as the Second Revised Affidavit of Authentication 
dated February 11;
4. The accompanying letter, identified by witness Calilung as not forming part of the 
record, transmitted by NBI Director Jaime C. Santiago to DOJ Prosecutor General 
Fadullon together with the second revised Affidavit of Authentication;
5. The DOJ Certification dated February 14, stating that the revised Affidavit of 
Investigation was sufficient for the conduct of a preliminary investigation; and
6. The Investigation Data Form dated March 7, noting that it had been prepared almost 
one month after the submission of the Affidavit of Authentication.

blocks.raw_text: In response. Presiding Officer Escudero explained that the Court possessed only those documents that had been pre-marked by the parties during pre-trial proceedings. He noted that some of the documents identified by Senator-Judge Marcos had not been pre-marked and had merely been used during the cross-examination of the prosecution witness. He assured Senator-Judge Marcos that the Court would provide whatever documents were available through the Office of the Clerk of Court, while emphasizing that such documents had not yet been formally offered in evidence by either party. Accordingly, he explained that the Court would still have to await the parties’ formal offer of evidence before determining their admissibility, if necessary. He assured Senator-Judge Marcos that the Court would endeavor to comply with her request to the fullest extent possible.
blocks.source_text:
In response. Presiding Officer Escudero explained that the Court possessed only those 
documents that had been pre-marked by the parties during pre-trial proceedings. He noted 
that some of the documents identified by Senator-Judge Marcos had not been pre-marked and 
had merely been used during the cross-examination of the prosecution witness. He assured 
Senator-Judge Marcos that the Court would provide whatever documents were available through 
the Office of the Clerk of Court, while emphasizing that such documents had not yet been formally 
offered in evidence by either party. Accordingly, he explained that the Court would still have 
to await the parties’ formal offer of evidence before determining their admissibility, if necessary. 
He assured Senator-Judge Marcos that the Court would endeavor to comply with her request to 
the fullest extent possible.

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) also proposed utilizing iPads or tablets, with the Secretariat, 
the Office of the Clerk of Court, and the senators' respective staff coordinating to facilitate access 
to the documents. To illustrate the need for a more efficient system, he pointed out that page 19 
of the Impeachment Complaint, specifically Footnote No. 72, referred to an affidavit allegedly 
contained in Annex G. He noted, however, that Annex G actually contained a certification and that 
the affidavit was instead located in Volume 14, Annex FFFFFFFFF to GGGGGGGGG. He further 
observed that the annexes had been assigned different labels when initially distributed and were 
later given new exhibit markings after being pre-marked, making the documents difficult to identify.

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) clarified that he was not requesting the advance disclosure of 
documents that might prejudice either party’s presentation or rebuttal. He proposed that copies, 
whether in digital or printed form, be furnished to the senator-judges as they are shown to the

proposing that the parties provide 24 printed copies of every document. Noting that the hearing was 
being held on a Wednesday, he suggested that, beginning the following day, the Office of the Clerk 
of Court coordinate with the respective staff of the Senator-Judges to develop an appropriate 
system for providing access to the documents.

In sum, the prosecution was right in using the term “culmination.” But the prosecution’s 
interpretation is wrong. Rather, when VP Sara left the Administration, she suffered various forms 
of threats and harassment for many months from the powers that be and the culmination was her 
statement on that fateful early morning in November 2024. She didn’t commit an impeachable 
offense. She drew the line in the sand and said, “Enough is enough.” That is the meaning and that 
is the context.

blocks.normalized_text: finally as to the alleged grave threats and inciting to sedition these are defined by the revised penal code and are punishable by prision correccional thus these fall under the jurisdiction of the mtc or first level courts these charges are not other high crimes as contemplated by the constitution as grounds for impeachment and are therefore not impeachable
blocks.raw_text: Finally, as to the alleged grave threats and inciting to sedition, these are defined by the Revised Penal Code and are punishable by prision correccional. Thus, these fall under the jurisdiction of the MTC or first level courts. These charges are not other high crimes as contemplated by the Constitution as grounds for impeachment and are therefore not impeachable.
blocks.source_text:
Finally, as to the alleged grave threats and inciting to sedition, these are defined by the 
Revised Penal Code and are punishable by prision correccional. Thus, these fall under the 
jurisdiction of the MTC or first level courts. These charges are not other high crimes as 
contemplated by the Constitution as grounds for impeachment and are therefore not 
impeachable.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge aquino appealed to both parties to conduct the examination of succeeding witnesses in a more direct and efficient manner observing that the testimony of the first witness had taken approximately nine hours to complete while recognizing that each party was entitled to pursue its own litigation strategy he urged counsel for both parties to allow the witnesses testimonies and documentary evidence to be presented more fully so that the issues could be better understood by the senator-judges and the public
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Aquino appealed to both parties to conduct the examination of succeeding witnesses in a more direct and efficient manner, observing that the testimony of the first witness had taken approximately nine hours to complete. While recognizing that each party was entitled to pursue its own litigation strategy, he urged counsel for both parties to allow the witnesses’ testimonies and documentary evidence to be presented more fully so that the issues could be better understood by the Senator-Judges and the public.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Aquino appealed to both parties to conduct the examination of succeeding 
witnesses in a more direct and efficient manner, observing that the testimony of the first witness 
had taken approximately nine hours to complete. While recognizing that each party was entitled to 
pursue its own litigation strategy, he urged counsel for both parties to allow the witnesses’ 
testimonies and documentary evidence to be presented more fully so that the issues could be better 
understood by the Senator-Judges and the public.

blocks.normalized_text: the prosecution informed the court that agent calilung would be its final witness for the day and that its next witness atty jeremy lotoc would be presented at the next hearing
blocks.raw_text: The prosecution informed the Court that Agent Calilung would be its final witness for the day and that its next witness, Atty. Jeremy Lotoc, would be presented at the next hearing.
blocks.source_text:
The prosecution informed the Court that Agent Calilung would be its final witness for the day 
and that its next witness, Atty. Jeremy Lotoc, would be presented at the next hearing.

blocks.normalized_text: upon clarification by the prosecution panel the schedule of witnesses for the following week was identified as follows atty lotoc on monday atty zuleika lopez on tuesday and capt belinda bello on wednesday while the presentation of nbi director melvin matibag would depend on the progress of the proceedings
blocks.raw_text: Upon clarification by the prosecution panel, the schedule of witnesses for the following week was identified as follows: Atty. Lotoc on Monday, Atty. Zuleika Lopez on Tuesday, and Capt. Belinda Bello on Wednesday, while the presentation of NBI Director Melvin Matibag would depend on the progress of the proceedings.
blocks.source_text:
Upon clarification by the prosecution panel, the schedule of witnesses for the following week 
was identified as follows: Atty. Lotoc on Monday, Atty. Zuleika Lopez on Tuesday, and Capt. 
Belinda Bello on Wednesday, while the presentation of NBI Director Melvin Matibag would depend 
on the progress of the proceedings.

blocks.source_text:

blocks.source_text: ORDERS OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

blocks.normalized_text: presiding officer escudero first informed the court that the box from the bureau of internal revenue bir which had previously been ordered returned had been delivered to the ber sealed unopened and duly acknowledged as received upon learning that the prosecution filed a motion for the issuance of a subpoena duces tecum and ad testificandum to the bir he instructed the parties to verify that respondent s counsel had been furnished a copy of the motion to facilitate its consideration
blocks.raw_text: Presiding Officer Escudero first informed the Court that the box from the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR), which had previously been ordered returned, had been delivered to the BER sealed, unopened, and duly acknowledged as received. Upon learning that the prosecution filed a motion for the issuance of a subpoena duces tecum and ad testificandum to the BIR, he instructed the parties to verify that Respondent’s counsel had been furnished a copy of the motion to facilitate its consideration.
blocks.source_text:
Presiding Officer Escudero first informed the Court that the box from the Bureau of Internal 
Revenue (BIR), which had previously been ordered returned, had been delivered to the BER sealed, 
unopened, and duly acknowledged as received. Upon learning that the prosecution filed a motion 
for the issuance of a subpoena duces tecum and ad testificandum to the BIR, he instructed the 
parties to verify that Respondent’s counsel had been furnished a copy of the motion to facilitate 
its consideration.

blocks.normalized_text: for ensure consistent treatment of the proposed subpoena for bank records and the request for the production of the bank records of the vice president and her spouse atty mans carpio presiding officer escudero directed both parties to submit their respective memoranda on the proposed bir subpoena by monday
blocks.raw_text: For ensure consistent treatment of the proposed subpoena for bank records and the request for the production of the bank records of the Vice President and her spouse, Atty. Mans Carpio, Presiding Officer Escudero directed both parties to submit their respective memoranda on the proposed BIR subpoena by Monday.
blocks.source_text:
For ensure consistent treatment of the proposed subpoena for bank records and the request for 
the production of the bank records of the Vice President and her spouse, Atty. Mans Carpio, 
Presiding Officer Escudero directed both parties to submit their respective memoranda on the 
proposed BIR subpoena by Monday.

blocks.normalized_text: he likewise scheduled oral arguments on both the bir records and bank records issues for wednesday allotting each party ten 10 minutes for its principal argument and an additional three 3 minutes for rebuttal after which the court would resolve the matter
blocks.raw_text: He likewise scheduled oral arguments on both the BIR records and bank records issues for Wednesday, allotting each party ten (10) minutes for its principal argument and an additional three (3) minutes for rebuttal, after which the Court would resolve the matter.
blocks.source_text:
He likewise scheduled oral arguments on both the BIR records and bank records issues for 
Wednesday, allotting each party ten (10) minutes for its principal argument and an additional three 
(3) minutes for rebuttal, after which the Court would resolve the matter.

blocks.raw_text: He further clarified that no subpoena would be issued to NBI Director Matibag for the following week’s hearings. Upon the prosecution’s manifestation, he instead directed the issuance of a subpoena to Atty. Zuleika Lopez requiring her to appear on July 13 and 14, noting that the July 15 hearing would be devoted to the scheduled oral arguments on the subpoena for the BIR and bank documents.
blocks.source_text:
He further clarified that no subpoena would be issued to NBI Director Matibag for the 
following week’s hearings. Upon the prosecution’s manifestation, he instead directed the issuance 
of a subpoena to Atty. Zuleika Lopez requiring her to appear on July 13 and 14, noting that the 
July 15 hearing would be devoted to the scheduled oral arguments on the subpoena for the BIR 
and bank documents.
